features.summary: Tesla delivered record Q4 2024 volumes at nearly 2 million annualized rate, with Model Y becoming the best-selling vehicle globally of any kind. The company reduced automotive costs below $35,000 per vehicle and achieved $2 billion quarterly free cash flow despite $2.4 billion annual CapEx increase. Energy storage reached all-time highs and remains supply constrained. Tesla will transition all factories to new Model Y production in February, causing temporary production disruption but positioning for future growth.
features.management_outlook: Management expects 2025 to be Tesla's most pivotal year, launching unsupervised Full Self Driving as paid service in Austin by June and expanding to multiple US cities by year-end. The company plans to produce several thousand Optimus robots internally in 2025, with external sales beginning in second half of 2026. Energy storage deployments expected to grow at least 50% year-over-year in 2025. CapEx will remain flat year-over-year as the company focuses on AI infrastructure and manufacturing preparation for what management calls an 'epic 2026' and 'ridiculous 2027-2028.'
features.risks: Battery production constraints limit current vehicle output growth, with management identifying this as the primary near-term bottleneck. Tariff uncertainty poses significant risks given global supply chain dependencies across all business segments. The Model Y factory retooling will result in several weeks of lost production and margin pressure in Q1 2025. Regulatory approval timelines for FSD deployment remain uncertain, particularly in Europe where bureaucratic processes could delay rollout until May 2026 or later.
features.key_drivers: Autonomous vehicle technology advancement represents the primary value driver, with management projecting FSD could enable 5x higher vehicle utilization rates and create the largest asset value increase in human history. Optimus humanoid robot development targets eventual $10+ trillion revenue opportunity with aspirational 10x annual production ramps. Real-world AI capabilities position Tesla significantly ahead of competitors, with management claiming no visible second place. The combination of autonomy, robotics, and energy storage creates multiple exponential growth vectors converging in the 2026-2028 timeframe.
